item.title: 12-hour funeral procession for slain leader through streets of Tehran underway
item.description: An estimated 15 to 20 million people, along with foreign dignitaries and religious leaders, were expected to take part in the ceremonies, which will last for several days.

item.title: FIFA criticized for lifting U.S. star's red card suspension after Trump phone call
item.description: Europe's soccer governing body and prominent commentators have criticized the decision, which allows Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium.

item.title: U.K. fighter jets intercept Russian plane near aircraft carrier off Iceland
item.description: The Russian Bear-F plane "repeatedly approached" the HMS Prince of Wales aircraft carrier, flying "unnecessarily close" and dropping sonar devices, according to the U.K.'s defense ministry.

item.title: Teen describes seaplane's rough landing in NYC's East River: "I thought we were going to drown"
item.description: The FAA is investigating after a hard landing by a seaplane that splashed down in the East River off Midtown Manhattan. Eight people were on board, including a 16-year-old who described the terrifying moment.

item.title: U.S. star's red card suspension lifted after Trump called FIFA president
item.description: Lead U.S. Men's National Team scorer Folarin Balogun is now eligible to play in Monday's match against Belgium after his one-game ban for a red card was lifted. CBS News confirmed President Trump called FIFA's president about the suspension. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: FIFA lifts Folarin Balogun's World Cup red card suspension after Trump's complaint
item.description: President Trump called FIFA President Gianni Infantino to discuss U.S. Men's National Team scorer Folarin Balogun's red card suspension match, sources say. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: Trump says "we don't want communists" in Fourth of July speech
item.description: In his Fourth of July speech on the National Mall, President Trump mentioned "communists" at least half a dozen times. He had similar messaging at Mount Rushmore on Friday. Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Prince Harry at odds with Buckingham Palace over U.K. stay
item.description: Prince's Harry's spokesperson says an offer to stay was "withdrawn at the last moment," which the Palace denies

item.title: You can now start contributing to a Trump Account. Here's what to know.
item.description: Starting July 4, people can deposit money into the new tax-deferred investment accounts, with eligible children receiving a $1,000 government contribution.

item.title: 2 National Guard members on patrol in Memphis fatally shoot man, police say
item.description: Two Tennessee National Guard members fatally shot a man in Memphis who turned toward them with a gun during a downtown pursuit, authorities said.

item.title: Millions brace for flooding after deadly heat, storms damper July 4th celebrations
item.description: While millions of Americans across the Northeast experienced record-setting temperatures, thunderstorms in the Midwest downed trees, ruptured power lines and made transportation treacherous.

item.title: Jewelry worth millions of dollars stolen from French museum
item.description: Burglars stole millions of dollars worth of jewelry from the museum of luxury glassmaker Lalique just months after a stunning gem heist at the Louvre.

item.title: China test-launches ballistic missile from sub in South Pacific, draws protests
item.description: China's military test-launched a long-range ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine in the South Pacific, drawing protest and concern from countries in the region.

item.title: Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine's capital kills at least 12
item.description: All the ballistic missiles launched by Russia struck their targets, underscoring Kyiv's need for more U.S. Patriot interceptor missiles, Ukraine officials say.

item.title: Balogun's red card suspension lifted after Trump called FIFA chief, sources say
item.description: Balogun's reinstatement came after President Trump called FIFA President Gianni Infantino last week to ask whether the organization was going to review the situation, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Telescope spots most distant quasars ever discovered
item.description: Quasars — the brightest objects in the universe — are powered by supermassive black holes at the heart of early galaxies.

item.title: Weather service faces hurricane season with less experienced staff, missing data
item.description: The National Weather Service is hiring hundreds of entry-level employees after losing about 15% of its staff to federal cuts last year.

item.title: Switching to a cheaper health plan delayed a man's access to vital medicine
item.description: A Florida man thought he had carefully planned for a seamless switch between health plans. It was anything but.

item.title: 5-day preliminary hearing for Charlie Kirk's alleged killer starts Monday
item.description: Prosecutors will present their case this week against Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Delta Air Lines flight hit by firework while landing at Midway International Airport
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight landing in Chicago was hit by a firework as it was coming to land at Midway International Airport on Saturday.

item.title: Seaplane carrying passengers makes hard landing in East River, FDNY says
item.description: A seaplane carrying at least eight people made a hard landing in New York City's East River on Sunday, officials said.

item.title: Money for repairs to national parks going to D.C. beautification projects
item.description: About 400 sites managed by the National Park Service are facing a maintenance backlog estimated at more than $24 billion, but the money aimed for repairs is being diverted. The Washington Post reports the Trump administration has used at least $90 million from national park entry fees to help pay for beautification efforts in the nation's capital ahead of the America 250 celebration. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Critical hearing this week for suspect in Charlie Kirk murder
item.description: Tyler Robinson, the man accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk, will be in court for a major hearing this week. Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Funeral underway for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: In Iran this weekend, millions took part in public funeral services for the country's slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and called for revenge for his death in a U.S. and Israeli airstrike. Leigh Kiniry has more.

item.title: Trump attacks Democrats in 4th of July speech marking 250 years of America
item.description: President Trump's speech marking America's milestone founding stressed patriotism and partisanship. He branded Democrats as communists after a series of victories by democratic socialist candidates across the country. Taurean Small reports.

item.title: 7/5/2026: Elemental Crisis; The Knowledge; Banana Ball
item.description: First, a report on the only active rare earth mine in the U.S. Then, London's cabbies skeptical about robotaxis. And, a look inside the world of Banana Ball.

item.title: 7/5: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Ed O'Keefe speaks to NCAA President Charlie Baker. Meanwhile, Reps. Adriano Espaillat and Carlos Gimenez discuss immigration and their American stories.

item.title: New evacuation orders after "significant growth" of Willow Fire
item.description: Authorities overseeing the firefight against the week-old Willow Fire west of Leadville issued new evacuation and pre-evacuation orders Sunday morning after the blaze grew by nearly 1,000 acres in 24 hours.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were married as they celebrated their wedding with hundreds of guests Friday at Madison Square Garden in New York City.
